message.content:
Done — I’ve set up the workflow for `tickets.csv`.

It will:

- Read the uploaded CSV file with expected columns:
  - `ticket_id`
  - `subject`
  - `body`
  - `customer_tier`
  - `amount`
- Send rows where `customer_tier == 'enterprise'` to:
  - `outputs/high_priority.jsonl`
- Send all other rows to:
  - `outputs/low_priority.jsonl`
- Quarantine any source-validation failures to:
  - `outputs/quarantine.jsonl`

I also set the JSON outputs to JSONL format and used `auto_increment` collision handling, so repeated runs won’t overwrite an existing output file.

Validation passed with no errors or warnings. State: **Structurally runnable** — the workflow is valid and runtime preflight passed; there were no field-level edge contracts to prove beyond the configured CSV source fields and routing rule.

metadata.name: Ticket Priority Split
metadata.description: Reads tickets.csv, routes enterprise customer_tier rows to high_priority JSONL and all other rows to low_priority JSONL, with invalid source rows quarantined.
